title: Yelp files antitrust lawsuit against Google
summary: Yelp has filed an antitrust lawsuit against Google, alleging that Google's dominant search market share illegally starves competitors of traffic and revenue. The lawsuit claims Google's search design prioritizes its own services and provides inferior results compared to specialized platforms like Yelp, ultimately harming consumers and businesses. Yelp is seeking damages and an injunction to address Google's alleged anti-competitive practices in local search advertising.

title: Duolingo director hacker sells $4.7m in stock
summary: Severin Hacker, Duolingo's Chief Tech Officer and Director, sold 9,000 shares of Class A Common Stock for approximately $4.7 million on June 20, 2025, while also exercising options to acquire 10,000 shares. This comes as Duolingo's stock experienced a decline due to Apple's new Live Translation feature, though analysts like JPMorgan, DA Davidson, and Scotiabank have raised price targets citing strong user growth and monetization strategies.

title: Alphabet’s Google Reaches $50M Settlement Over Claims of Systemic Racial Discrimination Against Black Employees
summary: Google has agreed to a $50 million settlement in a class-action lawsuit alleging systemic racial discrimination against Black employees, following a prior $28 million settlement for discrimination against non-white employees. The lawsuit claimed Google placed Black workers in lower-level roles, paid them less, and denied promotions, while using "coded language" like "Googley-ness" to marginalize them. These back-to-back settlements bring Alphabet's total discrimination-related payouts to $78 million in two months.

title: Is Marvell Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MRVL) a NASDAQ Stock with the Highest Upside Potential?
summary: Marvell Technology Inc. (NASDAQ:MRVL) is identified as a NASDAQ stock with significant upside potential, ranking first on a list compiled using analysts' upside potential and hedge fund sentiment. The article discusses Wedbush Securities' Dan Ives' concerns about tariffs and geopolitical tensions impacting tech supply chains and earnings, while highlighting Marvell's strong position in AI and cloud markets through partnerships like with Amazon Web Services. Bank of America reiterated a Buy rating on MRVL due to its AI opportunities and growth trajectory.

title: PayPal to lay off 2,000 employees in coming weeks, about 7% of workforce
summary: PayPal announced plans to lay off 2,000 employees, about 7% of its workforce, due to a "challenging macroeconomic environment." CEO Dan Schulman stated the company is focusing resources on core priorities and rightsizing its cost structure. This move follows a trend of recent job cuts across the tech industry, including Google, Microsoft, and Salesforce.

title: US OPEN: Selloff on Monday as Uncertainty Continues
summary: US markets experienced a selloff on Monday, with major indices showing negative performance and the VIX fear gauge climbing, indicating rising market anxiety. European markets also saw significant declines, while specific company news included Google Chrome divestiture pressures, plummeting Tesla China sales, Rocket Companies acquiring Redfin, and Robinhood settling with FINRA for $30 million.

title: Meta files lawsuit claiming AI company scrapes, sells Facebook users’ data
summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has filed a lawsuit against AI company Voyager Labs Ltd., alleging that Voyager created over 38,000 fake Facebook accounts to illicitly scrape data from more than 600,000 Facebook users. Voyager is accused of selling and licensing this stolen data for profit, marketing its software for "traceless collection from social media networks," and violating Meta's terms of service and California law. Meta claims Voyager's actions enabled secret surveillance on social media sites and highlights previous enforcement actions against the company.

title: Online retailer eBay is cutting 1,000 jobs. It’s the latest tech company to reduce its workforce
summary: Online retailer eBay is cutting approximately 1,000 jobs, or 9% of its full-time workforce, citing that its employee count and costs have outpaced its business growth in a slowing economy. This move follows a trend of layoffs across the tech industry, where companies that rapidly expanded during the COVID-19 pandemic are now trimming expenses. CEO Jamie Iannone informed employees that the company will also reduce its alternate workforce contracts and highlighted the need for better organization and nimbleness.

title: Ten things to know about the European Union's new product liability directive
summary: The European Union has passed a new product liability directive (PLD) which significantly expands the scope of product liability laws, impacting manufacturers of both consumer products and digital goods like software and AI. The directive introduces changes such as a shift in the burden of proof, extended liability periods, and new categories for recoverable damages, all aimed at modernizing product liability for the digital age and increasing consumer protection. Businesses operating in Europe face increased litigation risks and costs, with member states obligated to implement these changes into national law by December 2026.

title: Software company Autodesk lays off 250 employees
summary: Autodesk, a software company, has laid off 250 employees, which is less than 2% of its global workforce. This decision is part of its fiscal year 2024 plans to align resources with key priorities and comes amidst a global economic downturn affecting many tech companies. The affected roles include digital marketing, technical marketing, and web optimization, though the company continues to hire for other key positions.

title: General Motors hates your iPhone
summary: General Motors has decided to remove CarPlay and Android Auto support from its future electric vehicles, opting instead for its own Android Automotive-based infotainment system. This decision, driven by GM's desire for increased control over data and subscription revenues, is criticized for prioritizing corporate interests over user convenience and potentially degrading the in-car experience. The article raises concerns about app compatibility, data syncing, and the long-term software quality in GM vehicles, questioning whether other automakers will follow suit or leverage CarPlay as a competitive advantage.

title: Astera Labs: Connectivity Platform In AI World; Initiate With 'Buy' (ALAB)
summary: Astera Labs (ALAB) is initiated with a 'Buy' rating and a one-year target price of $110 per share, driven by its strong growth and innovative connectivity solutions critical for AI and cloud computing data centers. The company has demonstrated impressive 206% year-on-year revenue growth, with further expansion anticipated from new product releases like Scorpio X-Series and Taurus SCM. Key risks include manufacturing dependence on TSMC, competition from larger firms, and geopolitical factors affecting its presence in China.

title: Microsoft to shed 10,000 jobs, adding to glut of tech layoffs
summary: Microsoft announced it would eliminate 10,000 jobs and take a $1.2 billion charge to earnings as cloud-computing customers reduce spending and the company prepares for a potential recession. Despite this, Microsoft plans to increase investment in generative artificial intelligence, seen as a new growth area. The layoffs, affecting less than 5% of its workforce, are expected to conclude by the end of March, though strategic hiring will continue.

title: US stocks inch to more records as inflation slows and Oracle soars
summary: US stocks, particularly the S&P 500, reached new records on Wednesday, driven by an unexpectedly positive wholesale inflation report and Oracle's robust growth forecast fueled by AI demand. Despite a slight dip in the Dow Jones Industrial Average and a flat Nasdaq, the market remains optimistic about a Federal Reserve rate cut as inflation appears to be under control. Oracle's stock surged over 35% on news of multi-billion dollar AI contracts and massive projected cloud infrastructure revenue growth.

title: Earnings call transcript: Viant Technology Q1 2025 earnings beat expectations
summary: Viant Technology Inc. (DSP) reported a significant beat in its Q1 2025 earnings, with an EPS of $0.03 against a forecasted loss of $0.07 and revenue of $70.6 million, far exceeding expectations. The company saw a 32% year-over-year revenue increase and a 76% increase in adjusted EBITDA, driven by strong performance in connected TV (CTV) advertising and AI solutions. Viant's stock rose 4.96% in aftermarket trading, reflecting investor confidence and an outlook for continued growth, despite minor macroeconomic uncertainties causing some Q2 campaign activations to shift to the second half of 2025.

title: Stocks to Buy: 7 Founder-Led Companies Set to Outperform
summary: This article highlights seven founder-led companies that are expected to outperform the market, drawing on research that suggests such firms are more innovative and adaptable. It features Tesla (TSLA), Nvidia (NVDA), Figs (FIGS), Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (REGN), Crowdstrike Holdings (CRWD), On Holding (ONON), and Hyatt Hotels (H). Each company's unique strengths, market position, and growth potential under founder leadership are discussed, providing insights into why they are considered strong investment opportunities.

title: Abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund Q4 2024 Commentary (AOD)
summary: The abrdn Total Dynamic Dividend Fund (AOD) underperformed its benchmark in Q4 2024, primarily due to stock selection and sector allocation, with Tesla, Amazon, and Meta Platforms being key detractors. Despite global equity market gains driven by optimism over Trump's policies, the fund maintains a cautious outlook given macroeconomic factors and recession risks. The investment team focuses on stock-level diversification and capital preservation.

title: TSS Stock Jumps On AI-fueled Q1 Results, Strong Guidance: Retail Exuberance Abounds
summary: TSS, Inc. (TSSI) shares surged after reporting significant increases in Q1 2025 profits and revenue, driven by strong growth in procurement and systems integration, including AI rack integration services. The company's EPS reached $0.12, and revenue climbed 523% year-over-year to $99 million, leading to "extremely bullish" sentiment on Stocktwits and a 51.07% jump in after-hours trading. TSS also provided positive guidance for the remainder of the year and into 2026, expecting full-year 2025 adjusted EBITDA to be at least 50% higher than 2024.

title: Snapchat class action alleges social media network spams users with calls, texts
summary: A new class action lawsuit has been filed against Snapchat by an Oregon resident, Nathan King, alleging violations of the Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA). King claims Snapchat sent him numerous unsolicited text messages promoting content, despite his phone number being on the National Do Not Call Registry. The lawsuit suggests Snapchat uses these messages to inflate user numbers by redirecting users to download the app rather than directly accessing advertised content, and King seeks to represent other consumers who have experienced similar unsolicited messages.

title: Andreas Halvorsen Slashes Meta, Trims High-Beta Bets
summary: Andreas Halvorsen's Viking Global Investors LP significantly reduced its holdings in Q2 2025, signaling a shift away from high-beta growth stocks. The fund slashed its Meta Platforms (META) stake by almost 82% and fully exited positions in blue-chip companies like UnitedHealth Group (UNH), Intuit (INTU), and Netflix (NFLX). These moves indicate a cautious stance on tech valuations and a reallocation of capital.

title: ‘Apple’s Biggest Threat Is Not Google…’: Former Apple Chief Has This Warning For CEO Tim Cook - News18
summary: Former Apple CEO John Sculley warns current CEO Tim Cook that Apple's biggest threat is not Google or Meta, but OpenAI, due to its significant lead in the AI race. Sculley believes Apple is lagging in AI and needs to shift focus from apps to "Agentic AI" for future growth, suggesting a new CEO may be needed to navigate this era after Cook. He also notes delays in Apple's AI initiatives like Siri, leaving the company playing catch-up with rivals.

title: Growth Beats Yield at This T. Rowe Price Mutual Fund
summary: The T. Rowe Price Dividend Growth Fund (PRDGX) has lagged the S&P 500 over the past year due to its focus on dividend-growing companies rather than the mega-cap tech stocks that have driven market gains. Manager Tom Huber prioritizes companies with increasing dividends over high yields, anticipating a shift in investor attention towards dividend stocks as interest rates are expected to fall. He is currently investing in discounted sectors like consumer staples, energy, and healthcare.

title: Salesforce shares drop on slowest revenue growth in more than 10 years
summary: Salesforce Inc (CRM.N) shares dropped about 7% after the company reported its slowest quarterly revenue growth in over 10 years, attributing it to companies cutting back on cloud-based software spending amid an uncertain U.S. economy and weaker demand. Despite the slowdown, analysts remained largely positive, citing Salesforce's push into AI and improved profitability as factors for future growth, leading to several price target increases.

title: Nvidia surpasses Microsoft to become the world’s most valuable company
summary: Nvidia Corp. has surpassed Microsoft Corp. to become the world's most valuable publicly traded company, reaching a market capitalization of $3.34 trillion. This milestone follows a surge in Nvidia's stock, driven by its dominance in the AI chip market, particularly with its GPUs essential for generative AI development. The company's exceptional growth contrasts with Microsoft and Apple, which have also benefited from AI but not to the same extent, solidifying Nvidia's unique position in the tech landscape.

title: Where Will Apple (AAPL) Be in 5 Years? - AOL.com
summary: This article analyzes the future prospects of Apple (AAPL) over the next five years, suggesting that its stock may underperform the broader market despite its strong brand and ecosystem. The author highlights concerns about slowing sales growth and a high valuation (P/E ratio of 39.2) as key headwinds. While acknowledging Apple's past success and status as Berkshire Hathaway's largest position, the piece advises investors to temper expectations for market-beating returns in the coming half-decade.

title: Baron Technology Fund Q2 2025 Shareholder Letter
summary: The Baron Technology Fund significantly outperformed its benchmark, the MSCI ACWI Information Technology Index, during the second quarter of 2025, driven by strong gains from companies like Broadcom, NVIDIA, and Spotify. The fund emphasizes active management and investment in technology winners with sustainable competitive advantages, particularly those benefiting from transformative trends like AI and cloud computing. The letter discusses the impact of AI on the market, recent M&A activity, and the fund's strategic portfolio adjustments, including increased positions in semiconductor companies and sales due to regulatory concerns.

title: Will Warren Buffett Keep Selling Apple?
summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway recently reduced its stake in Apple, leading to speculation about whether further sales will occur. This article explores Buffett's history with Apple, current challenges facing the tech giant such as declining sales in China and competitive pressures, and the potential market reaction to Berkshire's next 13F filing. Analysts offer differing views on Apple's future, particularly with the upcoming integration of AI.

title: Meta Bows Down to EU Regulations After Apple And Google, Cut Subscription Prices for Ad-Free Facebook and Instagram
summary: Meta Platforms has significantly reduced the subscription fee for its ad-free Facebook and Instagram services in Europe, lowering the monthly cost to 5.99 euros from 9.99 euros. This move addresses concerns from privacy and antitrust regulators and comes as a response to the EU's Digital Markets Act (DMA). The company aims to find a middle ground amidst EU privacy laws and the DMA's stipulations, following similar concessions by Google and Apple.

title: Online retailer eBay is cutting 1,000 jobs. It’s the latest tech company to reduce its workforce
summary: Online retailer eBay is cutting approximately 1,000 jobs, or 9% of its full-time workforce, citing that its employee count and costs have outpaced its business growth in a slowing economy. This move follows a trend of layoffs across the tech industry, where companies that rapidly expanded during the COVID-19 pandemic are now trimming expenses. CEO Jamie Iannone informed employees that the company will also reduce its alternate workforce contracts and highlighted the need for better organization and nimbleness.

title: New Mexico accuses Snapchat of being ‘breeding ground’ for child predators
summary: New Mexico has filed a lawsuit against Snap Inc., accusing Snapchat of facilitating child sexual exploitation and becoming a "breeding ground" for predators due to its ephemeral messaging features. The state's attorney general, Raúl Torrez, stated that an undercover investigation found a "vast network" of dark websites sharing nonconsensual images from Snapchat. Snap Inc. is reviewing the complaint and maintains its commitment to making Snapchat a safe platform for young users, highlighting ongoing efforts to combat bad actors and enhance online safety tools.

title: Will India be able to deal with Google’s adtech monopoly?
summary: This article examines Google's monopolization of the adtech sector, detailing how the company's acquisitions and anti-competitive practices have harmed media and newspaper publishers globally, including in India. It discusses actions taken by regulatory bodies in the EU, UK, and US, and suggests that India's current competition regime needs significant amendments to effectively address Google's dominant position and ensure fair market practices. The piece highlights the dire financial consequences for publishers and the need for greater transparency and regulation in the digital advertising landscape.

title: Costco class action alleges retailer shared customer health info with Meta
summary: Costco is facing a class action lawsuit alleging that it shared customers' personal health information with Meta through embedded tracking tools like Facebook Pixel, without consent. The lawsuit claims that this data, gathered from Costco's pharmacy website where customers manage prescriptions, is not anonymous and is linked to specific Facebook user profiles. The plaintiffs argue that Costco's actions violate HIPAA regulations designed to protect private health information.

title: Apple class action allege App Store offers malicious crypto apps
summary: A class action lawsuit has been filed against Apple Inc., claiming the company authorized and maintained malicious crypto applications on its App Store, leading to the theft of consumers' personal financial assets. Plaintiffs allege Apple misrepresented the safety and legality of these apps, including Digicoins, SolLuna, and Forex5, which they assert were "spoofing" programs designed to steal account information. The lawsuit seeks to represent a nationwide class of consumers for alleged violations of California's Consumers Legal Remedies Act and Unfair Competition Law.

title: Snap Inc. (SNAP) Sued: Lawsuit Alleges Ad Platform ‘Execution Error’ Concealed From Investors, According to Hagens Berman
summary: Hagens Berman has initiated a securities class action lawsuit against Snap Inc. (NYSE: SNAP) and its executives, alleging they misled investors about the performance and growth prospects of its digital advertising platform. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased shares between April 29, 2025, and August 5, 2025, claiming Snap concealed a significant "execution error" related to a change in its ad platform. This alleged deception surfaced after Snap reported Q2 2025 results, missing analyst estimates and admitting to changes that reduced ad prices, leading to a 17% stock price drop.

title: Florida sues Roku for allegedly selling children’s data without consent
summary: Florida's Attorney General has filed a lawsuit against Roku, accusing the company of illegally collecting and selling the personal data of children without parental consent. The lawsuit claims Roku violated both the Florida Digital Bill of Rights and the Florida Deceptive and Unfair Trade Practices Act, seeking significant civil penalties and an injunction to stop these practices. The state alleges Roku ignored signs of child users and failed to implement adequate privacy controls, collecting data like locations, viewing habits, and voice recordings.

title: US stock market future slips as Trump’s 30% tariffs rattle global trade; Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq dip while Bo
summary: US stock futures slipped after President Donald Trump announced 30% tariffs on imports from the EU and Mexico, beginning August 1st, stoking global trade tensions. Investors are also closely watching second-quarter earnings reports from major banks, upcoming inflation data, and the growing rift between Trump and Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Despite the market dip, Boeing saw gains while Apple and Tesla slid, and Bitcoin hit a new record high.

title: United States Supreme Court to Hear Two Securities Cases This Term
summary: The Supreme Court is scheduled to hear two securities cases, Facebook, Inc. v. Amalgamated Bank and NVIDIA Corp. v. E. Ohman J:or Fonder AB and Stichting Pensioenfonds PGB, both concerning the pleading standard in securities fraud class actions. These cases will clarify whether companies must disclose past instances of risk materialization and further define the heightened pleading standards for scienter and falsity under the Private Securities Litigation Reform Act of 1995. The outcomes could significantly impact future securities fraud litigation and the role of expert opinions in such cases.

title: Will 2025 Be the Year of Diversification in Equities?
summary: As geopolitical tensions, trade policy shifts, and inflation remain high, 2025 is seen as a crucial year for portfolio diversification. The article highlights four key areas for investors to consider: diversifying within U.S. equities, exploring less-correlated international markets like Japan, utilizing broad commodity indices, and investing in the growing cryptocurrency ecosystem as regulatory frameworks evolve. This comprehensive approach aims to manage risk and address the dominance of U.S. equities and mega-cap concentration.

title: DOJ faces tough road in Apple antitrust lawsuit
summary: The Department of Justice's antitrust lawsuit against Apple faces significant challenges, despite drawing parallels to the successful Microsoft case two decades ago. Antitrust specialists highlight key differences and legal precedents that could make it difficult for the DOJ to prove Apple's monopolistic conduct and force product redesigns. Apple is expected to argue that its "walled-garden" approach benefits consumers and that the government's market definition is flawed.

title: Investors who lost over $250,000 with Apple Inc. (NASDAQ:AAPL) Shares Should Contact the Shareholders Foundation in Connection with Lawsuit
summary: The Shareholders Foundation, Inc. has announced a pending lawsuit for investors in Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) who purchased shares between June 10, 2024, and June 9, 2025, and lost over $250,000. The lawsuit alleges that Apple made misleading statements regarding the integration timeline of advanced AI-based Siri features for the iPhone 16. Investors are urged to contact the Shareholders Foundation by the August 19, 2025 deadline.

title: Swiss privacy tech firm Proton sues Apple in US over app store rules
summary: Swiss privacy software company Proton has filed a class-action lawsuit against Apple in U.S. federal court, accusing the tech giant of maintaining an illegal monopoly over iPhone app distribution and charging exorbitant commissions. Proton, known for its secure email service, seeks a court order to compel Apple to permit competing app stores and payment processors on its iOS platform. This action follows a related lawsuit filed by the Korean Publishers Association, highlighting ongoing antitrust concerns against Apple related to its App Store policies.

title: COLUMN: Live Nation's exploitative monopoly must end
summary: This column argues that Live Nation Entertainment's monopolistic control over concert ticketing, primarily through Ticketmaster, leads to exploitative practices for consumers. The author recounts personal negative experiences with the "Verified Fan" system for popular tours and details how Ticketmaster's practices, such as high fees, controversial insurance policies, and anti-competitive agreements with venues, restrict competition and harm fans. The article calls for the breakup of Live Nation Entertainment to foster a more equitable ticketing market.

title: Meta settles Israeli privacy lawsuit for $338,000
summary: Meta has settled an Israeli class-action lawsuit for $338,000, which will be transferred to the Israel Class Action Fund. The lawsuit alleged that Meta misused phone numbers provided for two-step verification by making them available to advertisers. As part of the settlement, Meta has revised its policies to ensure such phone numbers are used solely for security, safety, and integrity purposes.

title: Authors sue Meta, Microsoft, Bloomberg in latest AI copyright clash
summary: A group of authors including former Arkansas governor Mike Huckabee and Lysa TerKeurst have filed a class-action lawsuit against Meta, Microsoft, and Bloomberg, alleging unauthorized use of their copyrighted works to train AI systems. The lawsuit claims the companies used the "Books3" dataset, which contains pirated books, to train their large language models. The authors are seeking unspecified money damages and an injunction to prevent further misuse of their work.

title: Snapchat class action alleges social media network spams users with calls, texts
summary: A new class action lawsuit has been filed against Snapchat by an Oregon resident, Nathan King, alleging violations of the Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA). King claims Snapchat sent him numerous unsolicited text messages promoting content, despite his phone number being on the National Do Not Call Registry. The lawsuit suggests Snapchat uses these messages to inflate user numbers by redirecting users to download the app rather than directly accessing advertised content, and King seeks to represent other consumers who have experienced similar unsolicited messages.

title: Cloudflare net: CFO Seifert sells $12.1 million in stock
summary: Cloudflare's CFO, Thomas J. Seifert, sold 60,730 shares of Class A Common Stock for approximately $12.1 million on July 25 and 28, 2025, just before the company's scheduled earnings release. This sale occurred while the stock was trading near its 52-week high and, according to InvestingPro, appeared overvalued. The article also notes recent analyst upgrades for Cloudflare and a recent service outage.

title: Total Recall? What Infosec Teams Can Learn From Microsoft’s Misstep
summary: This article discusses the privacy concerns raised by Microsoft's "Recall" app, highlighting its initial "opt-out" implementation and the subsequent backlash. It explores the benefits of such AI-enabled tools for productivity while emphasizing the significant privacy risks, especially in enterprise settings. The author provides lessons for companies considering similar technologies, focusing on opt-in policies, risk assessment, secure configuration, data minimization, employee training, and incident response.

title: Twilio Under Investigation for Data Breach of Over 33 Million Authy MFA Users
summary: Schubert Jonckheer & Kolbe LLP is investigating a data breach affecting 33.4 million users of Twilio's Authy MFA app, where private data including phone numbers was accessed by third-party threat actors due to an unauthenticated API endpoint. The stolen data could be used for phishing, smishing, and SIM swapping attacks, and may enable further breaches. Additionally, Twilio is notifying customers about a separate breach involving an unsecured Amazon Web Services S3 bucket of a third-party vendor, which exposed SMS-related data.

title: HPE begins notifying data breach victims after Russian government hack
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) has started informing individuals impacted by a 2023 cyberattack, which the company attributes to Russian government hackers. The breach involved the theft of personal information, including Social Security numbers, driver’s license data, and credit card numbers, from HPE's email and SharePoint systems hosted by Microsoft. HPE confirmed the incident in January 2024, identifying the threat actor as "Midnight Blizzard" and noting that the stolen data primarily affected employees in cybersecurity and business teams, along with a small number of customers.

title: TRANSCRIPT: House Committee Hearing to Assess Microsoft’s Cybersecurity Shortfalls
summary: This transcript details a House Committee on Homeland Security hearing on June 13, 2024, where Microsoft President Brad Smith addressed the company's cybersecurity shortfalls, including the "Microsoft Exchange Online" intrusion and the SolarWinds attack. The hearing focused on the Cyber Safety Review Board's report, Microsoft's Secure Future Initiative, and the implications of the company's security culture for national security, with particular attention to state-sponsored threats from China and Russia. Smith accepted responsibility for the report's findings and outlined Microsoft's plans to enhance security, including tying senior executive compensation to cybersecurity performance.

title: Solar R&D innovation center advances PV technology
summary: First Solar has commissioned the largest thin-film R&D center in the Western Hemisphere in Lake Township, Ohio, investing $500 million in R&D infrastructure to accelerate innovation in PV technology. Meanwhile, talks between Google and cybersecurity firm Wiz for a $23 billion acquisition have stalled, and several R&D World Index members have experienced mixed fortunes.

title: Company and Personal Data Compromised in Recent Insight Partners Hack
summary: VC firm Insight Partners confirmed that a cyberattack discovered in January 2025 resulted in a data breach affecting both employees and partners. Impacted data may include fund, management company, portfolio company information, banking, tax, and personal details. The company is notifying affected entities and advising them to take protective measures such as changing passwords and monitoring financial accounts.

title: Solar R&D innovation center advances PV technology
summary: First Solar has commissioned the largest thin-film R&D center in the Western Hemisphere in Lake Township, Ohio, investing $500 million in R&D infrastructure to accelerate innovation in PV technology. Meanwhile, talks between Google and cybersecurity firm Wiz for a $23 billion acquisition have stalled, and several R&D World Index members have experienced mixed fortunes.

title: It's going to be a 'miserable summer' for CrowdStrike lawyers
summary: Following a significant outage, cloud cybersecurity company CrowdStrike faces class-action lawsuits from investors due to a more than 25% drop in stock value. While lawyers are actively seeking investors, customers may find it difficult to claim compensation for losses due to the company's terms and conditions. Analysts predict that this event will lead to increased scrutiny on quality assurance and staged rollouts for security content in the industry.

title: Apple pays $12.1 mln fine for alleged app market abuse in Russia - Antimonopoly Service
summary: Apple has paid a 906 million rouble ($12.12 million) fine to Russia's Federal Antimonopoly Service (FAS) over alleged abuse of its dominant position in the mobile apps market. The FAS had ruled in August 2020 that Apple's control over app distribution via its iOS operating system gave its own products a competitive advantage. This settlement follows a previous complaint from cybersecurity company Kaspersky Lab and comes after Apple paused product sales in Russia a year ago due to the conflict in Ukraine.

title: Microsoft Israel to lay off dozens as part of global cutbacks
summary: Microsoft Israel is preparing to lay off dozens of employees, impacting its cyber team for the first time, as part of Microsoft's broader global restructuring which includes 10,000 job cuts announced in January. The Israeli R&D center, employing about 2,700 people with half in cybersecurity, will see these layoffs primarily affect its security organization, with an estimated 10% reduction. This move follows other recent high-tech layoffs in Israel.

title: Data Breach Investigation on Cisco
summary: CYFIRMA's investigation revealed a significant data leak at Cisco involving the threat actor "IntelBroker," who claimed responsibility for compromising sensitive data including GitHub and GitLab projects, source code, hardcoded credentials, and customer information. This incident poses substantial security, reputational, and supply chain risks to Cisco and its B2B clients, as evidence suggests continued access despite Cisco's security measures. The breach also impacted numerous B2B clients, with potential exposure of their production source codes, and highlights the ongoing threat from the CyberNiggers hacking group.

title: HP Enterprise discloses hack by suspected state-backed Russian hackers
summary: Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE) revealed on Wednesday that suspected state-backed Russian hackers, identified as the group Cozy Bear, breached its cloud-based email system. The intrusion, discovered on January 12th, led to the exfiltration of data from the mailboxes of cybersecurity and other employees, although HPE states it has not materially impacted operations or financial health so far. This disclosure follows a similar report from Microsoft, which also blamed Cozy Bear for a breach starting in late November that accessed senior executive accounts.

title: Impact of Microsoft Copilot+ Recall on corporate cybersecurity
summary: Microsoft's Copilot+ Recall feature, which continuously captures screenshots and extracts information, poses significant cybersecurity risks for corporations, particularly regarding data retention and potential exfiltration. Despite Microsoft's revisions and delays, competitors like Apple and Google are developing similar AI-driven visual assistants, necessitating that IT and cybersecurity teams implement conservative policies and control measures. The article recommends a strategic approach, including policy creation, blocking spontaneous AI activation, and pilot programs to assess the benefits against the risks of these emerging technologies.

title: Delta Air Lines faces U.S. investigation over handling of system outage
summary: Delta Air Lines is under investigation by the U.S. Department of Transportation for its management of a recent system outage that resulted in thousands of flight cancellations. The airline has been disproportionately affected compared to other U.S. carriers, with operations severely disrupted due to its reliance on Microsoft's Windows operating system, which was impacted by a CrowdStrike software error. Delta is cooperating with the probe while focusing on restoring its services.

title: Synaptics CEO Says Chip Shortages Could Return Sooner Than You Think
summary: The CEO of Synaptics suggests that chip shortages, similar to those experienced in recent years, could resurface sooner than many industry observers anticipate. This article from Barron's highlights a technology CEO's perspective on the potential for renewed challenges in the semiconductor supply chain.

title: Accenture is cutting 75 San Jose workers
summary: Accenture is cutting 75 employees based at Google's campus in North San Jose, appearing to be cutting contractors who worked for Google. This news comes amidst a wave of other major layoffs by tech companies including First Republic, Google, Lyft, and Meta Platforms.

title: Synopsys Stock Falls on China Ban, But Long-Term Outlook Holds
summary: Synopsys (NASDAQ: SNPS) shares dropped after the Trump administration ordered a halt to EDA software sales to China, impacting about 10% of the company's revenue, though sales in the region were already declining. This ban also adds complexity to Synopsys's pending $35 billion acquisition of ANSYS (NASDAQ: ANSS), which still requires Chinese regulatory approval. Despite near-term uncertainty and a moderate "Hold" rating from analysts, the company's long-term outlook remains strong due to its indispensable role in chip design and the enduring secular trends in advanced chip development.

title: Unlocking The Potential Of Pure Research At InterDigital
summary: This article examines InterDigital's role as a pure research facility, led by CEO Liren Chen, and its significant contributions to wireless technology, including current work on 5G advanced and future plans for 6G with AI integration. It highlights InterDigital's impressive growth, long-standing partnerships with companies like Apple and Amazon, and its unique approach to fostering innovation among its largely inventor-based workforce. Chen emphasizes the importance of curiosity, competency, and impact in driving both individual and company success, showcasing InterDigital's continued influence on global digital communication.

title: Intel cuts more than 5,000 jobs across four states as restructuring accelerates
summary: Intel Corp. is laying off over 5,000 employees across four states, including California, Oregon, Arizona, and Texas, as part of a restructuring plan to cut over 20% of its global workforce. These job cuts aim to streamline management and achieve significant operating expense reductions. The layoffs are part of a broader trend of workforce reductions by major tech companies, amid concerns about AI's potential impact on employment.

title: Five9 Breaks General Policy to Quash Acquisition Rumors
summary: Five9 has issued a statement to deny recent market rumors, initially reported by Bloomberg, suggesting the company was exploring options for a sale. Although Five9 confirmed it was approached with an acquisition opportunity, it stated it is not pursuing any such deal. The article suggests that a Zoom acquisition is unlikely given Zoom's development of its own contact center platform and recent acquisitions, and speculates on other potential enterprise tech juggernauts if Five9 were considering an offer.

title: Google continued to ramp up federal lobbying spending before DOJ filed second antitrust lawsuit
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet, significantly increased its federal lobbying expenditures, spending over $13 million in 2022, prior to the Department of Justice filing its second antitrust lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges Google monopolized the digital advertising technology sector by acquiring rivals and manipulating the auction process to stifle competition. Despite these lobbying efforts and substantial political contributions, particularly to Democratic candidates, Alphabet also announced mass layoffs of 12,000 employees in January 2023.

title: Spotify to slash 6% of workforce, adding to tech layoffs glut | Daily Sabah
summary: Spotify announced it will lay off 6% of its 10,000 employees, amounting to about 600 people, as the music streaming giant prepares for a possible recession and aligns costs with revenue growth. CEO Daniel Ek admitted he was "too ambitious in investing ahead of our revenue growth." This move follows similar layoffs across the tech sector, including Google, Meta, Amazon, and Microsoft, as companies face economic headwinds and a downturn in demand after pandemic-driven growth.

title: Cognizant Technology Solutions to lay off employees tied to Apple in Austin
summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions announced layoffs impacting employees linked to Apple in Austin. The contractors affected will receive pay while exploring other positions within Cognizant. This move adds to a series of technology sector layoffs in Austin, though Central Texas is noted to be somewhat sheltered from broader industry cuts.

title: Tech giant with 30,000 employees has no return-to-office policy
summary: Globant SA, a software company with nearly 30,000 employees across 33 countries, has opted against a mandatory return-to-office policy, differentiating itself from other tech giants. CEO Martin Migoya emphasizes making offices attractive and flexible connection points rather than forcing attendance. The company is redesigning its office spaces to encourage collaboration and is planning to hire another 20,000 employees as part of a US$1-billion investment.

title: Zoom to lay off nearly 15% of staff as pandemic wanes
summary: Zoom Video Communications Inc. announced it will cut about 1,300 jobs, nearly 15% of its workforce, and incur related charges of up to $68 million as demand for its video conferencing services slows. CEO Eric Yuan will take a 98% pay cut and forego his bonus, while other executive leadership will see a 20% reduction in base salary. The company, which saw massive growth during the pandemic, is now reining in costs due to declining revenue growth, similar to other tech companies facing high inflation and rising interest rates.

title: Why is NICE’s share price tumbling?
summary: NICE’s share price is currently experiencing a downturn due to various factors, including the upcoming departure of veteran CEO Barak Eilam, increased competition from Microsoft, and economic uncertainty affecting new orders. The company's market cap has significantly decreased from its peak in 2021, and its ranking among leading Israeli companies has fallen. Despite this, analysts maintain a "Buy" recommendation with a high price target, and NICE has initiated substantial share buyback programs to boost investor confidence.

title: Snap CEO Evan Spiegel sells $1.57 million in company stock
summary: Snap Inc. CEO Evan Spiegel recently sold 150,000 shares of the company's Class A common stock for approximately $1.57 million. The sale was executed under a pre-arranged Rule 10b5-1 trading plan adopted in March 2023. Despite the sale, Spiegel retains a significant stake in the company, and recent financial evaluations by Fitch, S&P Global, and Moody's have assigned stable to positive outlooks for Snap's financial health and growth prospects.

title: Chipotle Plans to Hire 15,000 People Ahead of ‘Burrito Season’
summary: Chipotle Mexican Grill announced plans to hire 15,000 new workers in anticipation of its busiest period, dubbed "burrito season," which runs from March to May. This hiring spree contrasts with other large companies that are implementing layoffs. The company aims to nearly double its North American outlets to 7,000 and needs more staff to support this aggressive growth and manage increased sales during the spring.

title: News | Salesforce Seeks To Offload Large Block of Office Space in New Chicago Tower
summary: Salesforce is looking to sublease up to 125,000 square feet of its office space in the new 60-story Salesforce Tower in Chicago, citing recent job cuts and a desire to reduce real estate holdings. This move aligns with a broader trend of companies, including Meta, reducing their office footprints post-pandemic, while also highlighting the "flight to quality" as tenants seek premium spaces. The sublease offering is expected to attract high demand, given the limited availability of large, quality office spaces along the Chicago River.

title: Google continued to ramp up federal lobbying spending before DOJ filed second antitrust lawsuit
summary: Google's parent company, Alphabet, significantly increased its federal lobbying expenditures, spending over $13 million in 2022, prior to the Department of Justice filing its second antitrust lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges Google monopolized the digital advertising technology sector by acquiring rivals and manipulating the auction process to stifle competition. Despite these lobbying efforts and substantial political contributions, particularly to Democratic candidates, Alphabet also announced mass layoffs of 12,000 employees in January 2023.

title: Kroger workers who quit are getting texts and emails from the company asking them to come back
summary: Kroger, the largest supermarket operator in the U.S. by sales, is actively reaching out to former employees via texts and emails, encouraging them to return. This unusual recruitment strategy is a response to the current low unemployment rate and the competitive job market, with Kroger's chief people officer stating that "alumni are also a talent source." The company has found that returning workers are often more productive than new hires and has seen success in re-recruiting individuals, sometimes offering higher salaries.

title: Apple Kills Its Electric Car Project (Published 2024)
summary: Apple has canceled its decade-long project to develop an electric, self-driving car, a move announced internally to employees. This decision shifts many of the car project's staff to Apple's artificial intelligence division, marking a rare public setback for the company. The car, codenamed Titan, was intended to rival Tesla's E.V.s and bolster Apple's innovation legacy, but faced development challenges and financial investment of billions of dollars.

title: Cisco Talos report finds identity-based attacks drove majority of cyber incidents in 2024
summary: A new report from Cisco Talos indicates that identity-based attacks, leveraging valid credentials and old vulnerabilities, were responsible for 60% of cyber incidents in 2024. These attacks often exploited weaknesses in multi-factor authentication (MFA) and targeted Active Directory, with motivations ranging from ransomware to espionage. The report also noted that while AI use by threat actors was limited in 2024, it is expected to increase in 2025 across various attack phases and potentially target AI systems themselves.

title: Colorado Attorney General files lawsuit to block merger between Kroger and Albertsons
summary: Colorado Attorney General Phil Weiser has filed a lawsuit to block the proposed $24.6 billion merger between Kroger and Albertsons, parent companies of King Soopers, City Market, and Safeway. A year-long investigation concluded that the merger would eliminate competition, negatively impacting consumers, workers, and suppliers, and also challenged an illegal "no-poach" agreement from the 2020 King Soopers strike. While Weiser's office contends the merger would be detrimental, Kroger and Albertsons expressed disappointment in the lawsuit, arguing the merger would lead to lower prices, stronger union jobs, and counteract the dominance of non-unionized retailers like Walmart, Costco, and Amazon.

title: Meta says copying books was 'fair use' in authors' AI lawsuit
summary: Meta Platforms has argued in a U.S. court that its use of copyrighted books by authors like Ta-Nehisi Coates and Sarah Silverman to train its Llama AI system constitutes "fair use." The company is seeking to dismiss a lawsuit filed by the authors who claim Meta used pirated versions of their books without permission. Meta states its AI is transformative, not a substitute for reading the books, while the authors contend Meta systematically took expressive content without compensation.

title: Zoom Shares Surge on AI-Powered Earnings: ETFs to Gain
summary: Zoom Video Communications exceeded Q2 fiscal 2025 earnings and revenue estimates, driven by strong demand for its AI-powered collaboration tools and growth in enterprise customers, leading to a raised annual revenue forecast. Shares surged over 12% on the news, benefiting related ETFs like Global X Cloud Computing ETF (CLOU), First Trust Indxx Metaverse ETF (ARVR), and VictoryShares Free Cash Flow ETF (VFLO). Despite strong growth and an optimistic price target from analysts, the company faces challenges in maintaining momentum and intense competition.

title: Oracle Corp. stock rises Tuesday, outperforms market
summary: Oracle Corp. (ORCL) shares rose 0.82% to $89.70 on Tuesday, outperforming the broader market which saw mixed trading with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising and the S&P 500 falling. The stock achieved a new 52-week high and outperformed competitors like Microsoft and Alphabet. Trading volume was slightly below its 50-day average.

title: Why Mega-Cap Tech Stocks’ Dominance Is a Risk
summary: The "Magnificent 7" tech stocks now comprise a significant 30% of the S&P 500 Index’s market capitalization, creating concentration risks for investors. This historical top-heaviness makes portfolios vulnerable to significant drops if these stocks, highly correlated and sensitive to interest rates, decline. The article suggests strategies like investing in U.S. bonds, non-U.S. equities, equal-weighted S&P 500 strategies, and alternative assets to mitigate this risk.

title: Can iRobot bounce back? Deep concerns overshadow $127M Q2
summary: iRobot reported a significant revenue decline of 23.3% in Q2 2025, reaching $127.6 million, amidst ongoing challenges including slowing demand, increased competition, and the fallout from its failed Amazon acquisition. The company has suspended future financial guidance and is exploring strategic alternatives due to severe liquidity issues and a continuously declining cash position. Former CEO Colin Angle has also launched a new robotics startup, Familiar Machines & Magic, securing $15 million in funding.

title: Twilio Under Investigation for Data Breach of Over 33 Million Authy MFA Users
summary: Schubert Jonckheer & Kolbe LLP is investigating a data breach affecting 33.4 million users of Twilio's Authy MFA app, where private data including phone numbers was accessed by third-party threat actors due to an unauthenticated API endpoint. The stolen data could be used for phishing, smishing, and SIM swapping attacks, and may enable further breaches. Additionally, Twilio is notifying customers about a separate breach involving an unsecured Amazon Web Services S3 bucket of a third-party vendor, which exposed SMS-related data.

title: Apple Inc. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares fell 1.92% to $188.04 on Tuesday, marking its fifth consecutive day of losses. This underperformance occurred on a mixed trading day, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising and the NASDAQ Composite Index falling. The article highlights Apple's stock decline relative to its competitors and broader market movements.

title: Hims Drops Most Ever as Amazon Enters Hair-Loss Drug Market
summary: Hims & Hers Health Inc. shares experienced their largest-ever intraday drop (18%) after Amazon announced its entry into the hair-loss treatment market, a key segment for Hims. Amazon's increasing expansion into various consumer health sectors, including prescription drugs and telehealth services like One Medical, poses a significant competitive threat to Hims by offering similar convenience at competitive price points. This development highlights that despite Hims' recent focus on GLP-1 weight-loss drugs, hair loss and erectile dysfunction remain core to its business.

title: 5 Reasons to Avoid Microsoft (MSFT) Right Now
summary: This article outlines five reasons why investors should consider avoiding Microsoft (MSFT) stock at the moment, despite its historical success. The reasons include the stock being currently expensive compared to its historical price-to-earnings ratio, past unaddressed ethics issues, strong competition in its Azure cloud computing segment, potential for antitrust challenges, and the highly competitive nature of the generative AI market following its ChatGPT investment. However, the article also acknowledges Microsoft's long-term strength and suggests waiting for a stock pullback to a more favorable price-to-earnings ratio.

title: General Motors hates your iPhone
summary: General Motors has decided to remove CarPlay and Android Auto support from its future electric vehicles, opting instead for its own Android Automotive-based infotainment system. This decision, driven by GM's desire for increased control over data and subscription revenues, is criticized for prioritizing corporate interests over user convenience and potentially degrading the in-car experience. The article raises concerns about app compatibility, data syncing, and the long-term software quality in GM vehicles, questioning whether other automakers will follow suit or leverage CarPlay as a competitive advantage.

title: Amazon ends its charity donation program following other cost-cutting efforts
summary: Amazon is discontinuing its AmazonSmile charity donation program by February 20, 2023, as part of broader cost-cutting measures. Since 2013, the program has donated over $400 million to U.S. charities by giving 0.5% of eligible purchases. While Amazon states the impact was spread too thin, many small charities and shoppers have expressed dismay, noting the program's significant benefit to their operations.

title: Nvidia ETFS NVD, NVDQ Jump as Chipmaker Weighs Down QQQ, USD, SHOC
summary: Nvidia's stock decline is impacting technology ETFs like QQQ, USD, and SHOC, with the chipmaker approaching a correction after a significant rally. However, inverse single-stock ETFs such as NVDQ and NVD are experiencing gains. The market is also closely watching upcoming inflation data for clues on potential Fed rate cuts.

title: Oprah’s Dr. Laura Berman sues over son’s fentanyl death at 16: ‘Snapchat murdered Sammy’
summary: Dr. Laura Berman, a celebrity therapist, is suing Snapchat's parent company, Snap, Inc., after her 16-year-old son, Sammy, died from fentanyl poisoning after buying drugs from a dealer he connected with on the app. Berman and her husband are part of a larger group of 64 parents alleging product liability against Snap, pushing for "Sammy's Law" to allow parental monitoring of dangerous content on their children's devices. They argue that features like Quick Add and geo-location enable drug dealers to target minors, despite Snapchat's claims of enhanced safety measures and cooperation with law enforcement.

title: The Viral Cloudflare Termination Video Is a Master Class in How Not to Terminate Someone
summary: This article analyzes a viral video of a Cloudflare employee's termination, highlighting what went wrong and offering advice on how to properly terminate employees for performance issues. The author, an HR consultant, argues that the termination was poorly handled because the employee's direct supervisor was not involved, the HR representatives couldn't provide specific reasons for the performance issues, and the employee had no prior warning or documentation of poor performance. The piece concludes with a guide on how to conduct performance-related terminations professionally and legally.

title: PLDT warns public on AI-generated scams
summary: PLDT and Smart Communications are warning the public about the rise of AI-generated scams, which are becoming increasingly sophisticated and convincing. Criminals are leveraging AI to create polished messages and visuals that mimic official communications, making it harder for individuals to distinguish genuine messages from fraudulent ones. PLDT advises checking the source of emails and texts and adhering to the basic rule of not communicating with strangers to avoid falling victim to these scams.

title: Lessons for ETF Investors From Winners and Losers of DeepSeek AI Stock Selloff
summary: The emergence of DeepSeek AI's new model has caused a divergence in performance among AI-focused ETFs, highlighting the importance of understanding a fund's strategy. While hardware-centric AI stocks experienced a selloff, software-oriented funds prevailed, demonstrating how technological advancements can reshape investment landscapes in the AI sector. Investors continued to show confidence in the long-term potential of AI, as evidenced by net inflows into US-domiciled AI and Big Data funds despite the broad market shifts.

title: US investor Michael Burry makes US$10m bet on gold
summary: Renowned US investor Michael Burry, through his hedge fund Scion Asset Management, invested US$10 million in Sprott Physical Gold Trust in the first quarter of this year, acquiring 440,000 units. This significant bet on gold, now valued at approximately US$8.1 million, signifies Burry's confidence in the commodity as a hedge against economic uncertainty. His portfolio recalibration also involved shifting towards physical commodities and renewable energy while selling off positions in several tech giants and other retail/travel companies.

title: FBY: Far From Being An Optimal Strategy For Holding META (NYSEARCA:FBY)
summary: The article evaluates the YieldMax META Option Income Strategy ETF (FBY) as a strategy for holding Meta Platforms (META) stock, noting that while FBY generates high distributions through options, it caps upside potential and exposes investors to full downside risk. The author concludes that FBY significantly underperformed META shares on a total return basis and recommends a "SELL" rating for FBY, advocating for direct investment in META for better overall returns due to FBY's risks, value decay, and lack of diversification.

title: June Patch Tuesday Update - Critical Fixes & Vulnerabilities | Absolute Security Blog
summary: Microsoft's June Patch Tuesday includes 66 security fixes, with 10 critical vulnerabilities and several weaponized threats. The article highlights five top vulnerabilities, including critical remote code execution and elevation of privilege flaws in Power Automate, WebDAV, SharePoint, Windows SMB Client, and Windows RRAS. It stresses the importance of a resilient patching strategy and prioritizing immediate remediation for these high-severity issues.

title: Op-Ed: Why AI Companies Should Pay Media Organizations For Their Content
summary: The article argues that AI companies should pay media organizations for using their content, citing The New York Times' licensing deal with Amazon as a successful example. It highlights the shift from AI companies using copyrighted material without permission to paying for intellectual property, which creates accountability and helps publishers struggling with traffic. The author suggests that more artists and publishers should pursue similar deals to protect their work and ensure they are compensated for their contributions to AI training.

title: Microchip Technology Says a Cyber Breach Has Slowed Operations
summary: Microchip Technology Inc. experienced a cyberattack that compromised some of its servers and business operations, forcing the company to slow down its activities. The incident, detected on August 17, 2024, has impacted the US chipmaker, which also supplies the defense industry. The company confirmed the breach two days later.

title: QBIG Stock Price | Invesco Top QQQ ETF
summary: This page provides comprehensive information on the Invesco Top QQQ ETF (QBIG), including its current stock price, trading exchange, and key financial statistics. It also features performance data, holdings breakdown, company profile, and news related to the ETF, along with technical analysis insights.

title: These 4 major companies announced store closings in April
summary: Four major companies, including Monster Beverage Corp., JCPenney, Hudson's Bay Company, and Joann Fabrics, announced store closings in April. Monster Beverage is shutting down its Salt Lake City brewing facility, JCPenney is closing several stores in seven states, Hudson's Bay Company is liquidating all its remaining stores, and Joann Fabrics is closing all 800 of its locations after filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy. These closures are due to various reasons, from lease non-renewal to bankruptcy filings.

title: Proposal for new Costco store in region takes a step forward
summary: A proposal to bring a new Costco Wholesale Corp. store to Indian Land, S.C., has moved closer to reality after a second reading of a rezoning request was approved. The Lancaster County Council approved the request for a 28-acre site adjacent to The Exchange at Indian Land, a mixed-use development by Crosland Southeast. Three readings are required for final approval of the rezoning request.

title: Tech Stocks Are Still Pricey. Dip Buyers Should Look Here Instead.
summary: Investors seeking "buy-the-dip" opportunities might be advised to look beyond tech stocks, which remain expensive despite recent market fluctuations. Instead, the article suggests considering other sectors, highlighting that the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund has seen a significant drop from its recent high. This implies that industrial stocks may present a more attractive dip-buying opportunity than pricey tech stocks.
